# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a developer exploring `jj`'s ability to handle multi-parent merges (octopus merges). A repository already exists at `/home/user/octopus_repo` with a base commit containing a file `base.txt`. Your task is to create 3 concurrent bookmarks named `branch_a`, `branch_b`, and `branch_c` from the base commit. In each bookmark, add a new file (`a.txt`, `b.txt`, `c.txt` respectively) with some content. Finally, create a new commit that has all 3 bookmarks as parents, and bookmark it as `octopus_merge`. Verify the merge by checking the parents of `octopus_merge` and ensuring all files are present in the working copy.
I'll explore the repository and then set up the octopus merge step by step.

todos.content: Create 3 bookmarks (branch_a, branch_b, branch_c) from the base commit with new files
Create octopus merge commit with all 3 bookmarks as parents
Verify merge parents and file presence in working copy

output.output:
Working copy changes:
A base.txt
Working copy  (@) : ynwutkuy 6aeca5d9 base
Parent commit (@-): zzzzzzzz 00000000 (empty) (no description set)

parts.text: The repository has a single base commit (`ynwutkuy`) with `base.txt`. Now I'll create the three bookmarks from this base commit.

todos.content: Create 3 bookmarks (branch_a, branch_b, branch_c) from the base commit with new files
Create octopus merge commit with all 3 bookmarks as parents
Verify merge parents and file presence in working copy

parts.text: First, I need to commit the base change so it's immutable, then create branches from it.

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && jj bookmark create base -r ynwutkuy

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to ynwutkuy 6aeca5d9 base | base

parts.text: Now I'll create `branch_a` — a new commit on top of the base with `a.txt`:

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && jj new ynwutkuy -m "Add a.txt on branch_a"

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: owprpurq 2095ac5b (empty) Add a.txt on branch_a
Parent commit (@-)      : ynwutkuy 6aeca5d9 base | base

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && echo "content from branch a" > a.txt && jj bookmark create branch_a -r @

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to owprpurq 2680f2ba branch_a | Add a.txt on branch_a

parts.text: Now create `branch_b` — another new commit on top of the base with `b.txt`:

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && jj new ynwutkuy -m "Add b.txt on branch_b"

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: uvpkuyny f35016c6 (empty) Add b.txt on branch_b
Parent commit (@-)      : ynwutkuy 6aeca5d9 base | base
Added 0 files, modified 0 files, removed 1 files

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && echo "content from branch b" > b.txt && jj bookmark create branch_b -r @

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to uvpkuyny 20c90848 branch_b | Add b.txt on branch_b

parts.text: Now create `branch_c` — a new commit on top of the base with `c.txt`:

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && jj new ynwutkuy -m "Add c.txt on branch_c"

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: qsuyvwxq 4f351205 (empty) Add c.txt on branch_c
Parent commit (@-)      : ynwutkuy 6aeca5d9 base | base
Added 0 files, modified 0 files, removed 1 files

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && echo "content from branch c" > c.txt && jj bookmark create branch_c -r @

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to qsuyvwxq 59515f4c branch_c | Add c.txt on branch_c

parts.text: All three bookmarks are created. Let me verify the graph so far:

output.output:
@  qsuyvwxq test@example.com 2026-03-12 04:09:36 branch_c 59515f4c
│  Add c.txt on branch_c
│ ○  uvpkuyny test@example.com 2026-03-12 04:09:31 branch_b 20c90848
├─╯  Add b.txt on branch_b
│ ○  owprpurq test@example.com 2026-03-12 04:09:26 branch_a 2680f2ba
├─╯  Add a.txt on branch_a
○  ynwutkuy test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:32:19 base 6aeca5d9
│  base
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: All three branches fork from the base commit. Now I'll create the octopus merge.

todos.content: Create 3 bookmarks (branch_a, branch_b, branch_c) from the base commit with new files
Create octopus merge commit with all 3 bookmarks as parents
Verify merge parents and file presence in working copy

input.command: cd /home/user/octopus_repo && jj new branch_a branch_b branch_c -m "Octopus merge of branch_a, branch_b, and branch_c"

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: lqszuquk ce518d37 (empty) Octopus merge of branch_a, branch_b, and branch_c
Parent commit (@-)      : owprpurq 2680f2ba branch_a | Add a.txt on branch_a
Parent commit (@-)
